disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Facial redness came on over about 4 hours this afternoon, directly following sun exposure while gardening, and is hot and burning
trigger_question_examples: When and how did the redness start?
Were you doing anything just before it came on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started this afternoon after I'd been out gardening in the sun for a while — it came on over a few hours and it feels hot and burning.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The redness is across both cheeks and over the top of the nose
trigger_question_examples: Is it on one side or both sides of your face?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's on both my cheeks and across the top of my nose — it's fairly even on both sides.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever or chills; the patient has checked their temperature and it is normal
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fever, chills or sweats?
Have you been able to check your temperature?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever — I checked and my temperature is normal, and I don't feel hot or shivery.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No shortness of breath, no chest pain, no joint swelling; passing urine normally and urine is a normal colour
trigger_question_examples: Any chest pain, trouble breathing, or joint swelling?
Any change in how much you're passing urine, or its colour?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — my breathing is fine, no chest pain, my joints are okay, and I'm passing urine normally and it looks normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient was told a few years ago they have a positive ANA test and possible lupus, but has been asymptomatic until now
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any medical conditions?
Has a doctor ever mentioned anything about your immune system or blood tests?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: A few years ago a doctor said I had a positive ANA and possible lupus, but I've never had any symptoms from it before today.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The skin is not weeping, blistering, oozing pus or broken open; there are no obvious pus spots
trigger_question_examples: Is the skin blistering, weeping or breaking open?
Can you see any pus, crusting or open sores?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, the skin isn't broken or weeping and there's no pus — it's just red and hot to touch.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient is not currently using any sunscreen or skin creams
trigger_question_examples: Do you use sunscreen or any creams on your skin?
Were you protected from the sun today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I wasn't wearing any sunscreen and I don't use any creams — I was just out in the garden.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient is highly anxious and fears the redness means a severe internal flare attacking their organs
trigger_question_examples: What is worrying you most about this?
What were you hoping we could sort out today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm really frightened — I keep thinking this means my lupus is flaring badly and my organs are being damaged.
